level_signals: 6+ years of experience in sales with enablement and quota-carrying experience
Expected to own and maintain sales playbooks from scratch
Responsible for program design and cross-functional stakeholder management

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: you should bring 6+ years of experience in sales, ideally with both enablement and quota-carrying experience within a high-growth technology environment.
Strong understanding of the account management and sales environment including methodologies, processes, and CRM platforms
Specific knowledge of Salesforce, Salesloft, and other related tools to expertly manage sales cycles
Experience with sales enablement tools such as Gong, Salesloft, or similar
Hands-on experience with a Learning Management System (e.g., WorkRamp or equivalent)—including building content libraries, assigning learning paths, and tracking completion and performance metrics

Demonstrated ability to write, own, and maintain sales playbooks from scratch—including call frameworks, objection handling guides, discovery flows, and structured deal strategies for mid-market and/or enterprise segments
Strong project management, organization, and stakeholder engagement skills, including multitasking to lead and execute multiple projects in parallel
Exceptional communication, facilitation, and presentation skills, with the ability to clearly articulate ideas and concepts to diverse audiences

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience supporting or selling into mid-market and/or enterprise accounts is a plus—familiarity with structured deal processes, multi-stakeholder cycles, and longer sales motions will be leveraged directly in enablement design

scope.surface: Sales and Account Management revenue enablement — training programs, playbooks, onboarding, LMS, and enablement infrastructure
primary_metrics_inferred: Time to productivity for new hires
Enablement program KPIs and completion rates
Sales team performance and pipeline metrics
Knowledge/skill gap reduction across account management team
core_responsibilities: Support the enablement and overall success of revenue generating teams in partnership with leadership
Develop, own, and continuously maintain sales playbooks that reflect current go-to-market strategy, product positioning, and deal execution
Leverage sales technology platforms to build scalable enablement infrastructure including Gong, Salesforce, and Salesloft
Serve as a key liaison between revenue teams, sales operations, marketing, and product
Administer and optimize a Learning Management System (LMS) such as WorkRamp
Create, deliver, and continuously improve onboarding programs for new hires
Analyze enablement program effectiveness through KPIs and feedback
Use performance data to identify knowledge or skill gaps across the account management team

green_flags: Named Forbes #1 Best Startup Employer in America — strong employer brand signal
Clear list of tools and technologies expected (Gong, Salesforce, Salesloft, WorkRamp) — well-defined role scope
Specific responsibilities are well articulated and actionable
Generous benefits package including parental leave, family planning, wellness, and dogfooding budget
Multiple hub city options (NYC, LA, SF, London) provide geographic flexibility within hybrid constraint
red_flags: Role described as 'remote co-located' company-wide, but this specific role requires commuting distance to a hub — potential candidate confusion
Quota-carrying experience listed as 'ideally' but is framed as a soft qualifier inside what reads like a hard requirement paragraph — ambiguous classification

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 6+ years requirement and scope of ownership)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly stated)
ambiguities: The JD introduction describes Whatnot as a 'remote co-located team' with hubs, suggesting broad remote flexibility, but the role body explicitly states 'Team members in this role are required to be within commuting distance' of specific hubs — treated as hybrid onsite-required here.

The 6+ years requirement uses 'ideally with both enablement and quota-carrying experience' — 'ideally' could soften the quota-carrying component to nice-to-have, but the overall 6+ years framing was treated as a hard requirement. The quota-carrying qualifier remains in the severity:hard item but noted as partially softened by 'ideally'.
'1212' appears in the JD body near the benefits section — likely a formatting artifact or internal code; no structured meaning extracted.
